Description:
Scaffold the Next.js 16 frontend with static export, Tailwind 4, and MUI 7. Build the core UI components and dashboard page that lists jobs and triggers the scraper API.

SCOPE:
- Next.js 16 scaffold with output: export, Tailwind 4, MUI 7, TanStack Query, Zustand, Zod
- JobCard, FilterBar, and ScrapeButton components
- Dashboard page: job listing with filter and scrape trigger
- Frontend AGENT.md documenting component conventions

MILESTONE: MVP 1 — Scraper Foundation & In-Memory Store

Initialise the frontend/ package inside the monorepo using Next.js 16 with static export configuration.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Scaffold frontend/ with: Next.js 16, TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS 4, MUI 7, TanStack Query v5, Zustand, Zod
- next.config.ts: output: 'export', basePath and trailingSlash set as per proposal-v4-structure.md
- tsconfig.json: strict: true, paths alias @/* -> ./src/*
- package.json scripts: dev, build, lint, type-check (tsc --noEmit)
- Create frontend/AGENT.md documenting: component file conventions, import alias usage, API base URL from env, Tailwind class ordering

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- pnpm build produces out/ static export directory without errors
- tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
- ESLint passes with zero warnings
- next.config.ts output: export confirmed in build output log

EDGE CASES:
- Static export incompatible with Next.js Image Optimization; configure unoptimized: true in next.config.ts
- MUI and Tailwind class conflicts; configure Tailwind prefix or use MUI sx prop as primary styling

Create frontend/components/JobCard.tsx to display a single job listing.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Props: job: Job (imported from shared types or local interface matching backend Job model)
- Display fields: title (heading), company, location, source badge (chip coloured by source_id), relevance_score (percentage bar or badge), salary range (if present), scraped_at (relative time)
- Link: clicking card opens job.url in new tab
- Accessibility: role='article', aria-label with job title and company
- Styling: Tailwind utility classes; MUI Card as base component
- Responsive: full width on mobile, grid item on desktop

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-4-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Renders correctly with all fields populated
- Renders correctly with optional salary fields absent
- Source badge colour differs per source_id value
- Clicking card opens correct URL
- No TypeScript errors on tsc --noEmit

EDGE CASES:
- Very long job titles must truncate with ellipsis, not break layout
- relevance_score of 0.0 renders as 0% not blank

Create frontend/components/FilterBar.tsx for client-side source and keyword filtering.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Props: onFilterChange: (filters: FilterState) -> void
- FilterState type: { sources: string[]; keyword: string }
- Source filter: multi-select checkboxes for each unique source_id in current job list; sourced from Zustand store
- Keyword filter: text input with debounce (300ms) triggering onFilterChange
- Clear filters button: resets all filter state
- Styling: horizontal bar on desktop, collapsible panel on mobile

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-4-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Selecting a source checkbox filters displayed jobs by that source
- Typing in keyword input filters by title and description match (client-side)
- Clear button resets all filters and shows full job list
- Debounce prevents excessive re-renders on fast typing
- tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors

EDGE CASES:
- No jobs loaded yet: source checkboxes render empty state gracefully
- Keyword filter is case-insensitive to match backend filter behaviour

Create ScrapeButton component and wire up the main dashboard page to display jobs and trigger scraping.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- ScrapeButton (frontend/components/ScrapeButton.tsx):
  - On click: POST /api/v1/scrape with current keyword filter as body
  - In-progress state: disabled button with spinner and 'Scraping...' label
  - On success: invalidate TanStack Query jobs cache to trigger refetch
  - On error: display toast notification with error message
- Dashboard page (frontend/app/page.tsx):
  - TanStack Query: useQuery fetching GET /api/v1/jobs; refetch every 60s
  - Render FilterBar, ScrapeButton, and filtered list of JobCard components
  - Empty state: 'No jobs found. Click Scrape to fetch latest listings.'
  - Loading skeleton: show 6 placeholder cards while fetching
- API base URL: read from NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL env variable

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-4-2, JD-4-3

TESTING CRITERIA:
- POST /api/v1/scrape called on button click with correct body
- Button disabled and shows spinner during in-flight request
- Job list refreshes after successful scrape
- Empty state renders when jobs array is empty
- Loading skeletons visible on initial page load

EDGE CASES:
- API unreachable: display error banner, do not crash page
- Scrape in progress while filter changes: filter applies to current job list without cancelling scrape

Implement the jobs REST API with cursor-based pagination, OpenAPI typed schemas, admin utility scripts, and a GitHub Actions CI pipeline covering lint, type checks, tests, and Docker build.

SCOPE:
- GET /api/v1/jobs with cursor pagination and GET /api/v1/jobs/{id}
- All routes typed with Pydantic request/response models; auto-generated OpenAPI docs
- Admin scripts: seed_keywords.py, clear_db.py, stubs for DLQ and evals
- GitHub Actions CI: Ruff, mypy, ESLint, tsc, pytest, Vitest, Docker build

MILESTONE: MVP 1 — Scraper Foundation & In-Memory Store

Create backend/routers/jobs.py implementing the jobs REST API with cursor-based pagination.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Router: APIRouter(prefix='/api/v1', tags=['jobs'])
- GET /jobs:
  - Query params: cursor (Optional[str], default None), limit (int, default 20, max 100), source (Optional[str]), keyword (Optional[str])
  - Cursor: base64-encoded job ID marking start of next page
  - Apply source filter and keyword filter (calls filters.filter_jobs) before pagination
  - Response model: JobsPage { items: list[Job]; next_cursor: Optional[str]; total: int }
- GET /jobs/{job_id}:
  - Calls get_job(job_id) from fake DB
  - Returns Job or 404 HTTPException with message 'Job not found'
- Include router in main.py

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2-6, JD-2-5

TESTING CRITERIA:
- GET /api/v1/jobs returns 200 with JobsPage schema
- Cursor pagination: second request with next_cursor returns next page
- Source filter returns only matching source jobs
- Keyword filter returns only matching jobs
- GET /api/v1/jobs/unknown-id returns 404

EDGE CASES:
- limit capped at 100 to prevent memory exhaustion
- Invalid cursor value returns 400 with clear error message
- Empty store returns items: [], next_cursor: null, total: 0

Ensure all API routes have complete Pydantic request and response models for auto-generated OpenAPI documentation.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Define explicit response_model on every route decorator (no untyped dict returns)
- Create backend/schemas.py for request/response schemas not covered by models.py:
  - ScrapeRequest: { keywords: list[str] }
  - JobsPage: { items: list[Job]; next_cursor: Optional[str]; total: int }
  - HealthResponse: { status: str; version: str }
- Add tags, summary, and description strings to all route decorators
- Verify /docs renders all endpoints with correct request and response schemas

EFFORT: S (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-5-1, JD-3-5

TESTING CRITERIA:
- GET /openapi.json returns valid OpenAPI 3.1 spec
- All endpoints listed in /docs with correct schemas
- No Any types in response models
- tsc --noEmit equivalent: uv run python -m mypy backend/ --ignore-missing-imports passes

EDGE CASES:
- Circular schema references between Job and ScrapeResult must be resolved with model_rebuild()

Create admin utility scripts in backend/admin/ for database management and operational tasks.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- backend/admin/seed_keywords.py:
  - Accepts --keywords arg (comma-separated list)
  - Updates settings-compatible keyword list (writes to .env or prints export commands)
  - Logs seeded keywords via get_logger
- backend/admin/clear_db.py:
  - Clears jobs_store dict in MVP 1 (prints warning about in-memory limitation)
  - Stub comment: # MVP 2: execute DELETE FROM jobs via asyncpg
- backend/admin/replay_dlq.py (stub):
  - Prints 'DLQ replay not implemented until Epic 10 (MVP 2)'
- backend/admin/run_evals.py (stub):
  - Prints 'Eval runner not implemented until Epic 7 (MVP 1.1)'
- All scripts use argparse and get_logger

EFFORT: S (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-2-3

TESTING CRITERIA:
- seed_keywords.py runs without errors and logs seeded list
- clear_db.py runs without errors and prints MVP 1 warning
- Stub scripts exit 0 with informative messages
- All scripts importable without side effects (if __name__ == '__main__' guard)

EDGE CASES:

Create .github/workflows/ci.yml implementing the full CI pipeline for the monorepo.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Trigger: on push and pull_request to main branch
- Jobs:
  - backend-lint: uv run ruff check backend/; uv run mypy backend/ --ignore-missing-imports
  - backend-test: uv run pytest backend/tests/ -v --tb=short
  - frontend-lint: pnpm eslint frontend/src/; pnpm tsc --noEmit
  - frontend-test: pnpm vitest run
  - docker-build: docker build -t job-discovery . (build only, no push on PR)
  - check-workflow-docs: fail if tasks/todo.md or tasks/lessons.md missing from repo (per E16)
- Cache: pip/uv cache and pnpm store cached between runs
- All jobs run in parallel except docker-build which depends on backend-lint and frontend-lint

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- CI triggers on PR to main
- Ruff lint failure blocks merge
- mypy type errors block merge
- ESLint and tsc errors block merge
- Docker build failure blocks merge
- check-workflow-docs fails if todo.md absent

EDGE CASES:
- Monorepo path filtering: backend changes should not trigger frontend-only jobs unnecessarily
- Secrets not required for CI (no cloud credentials needed for MVP 1 lint/test/build)

Establish the workflow tracking, documentation, and CI enforcement infrastructure that governs how the AI agent and human developers plan, execute, verify, and learn from every implementation step.

SCOPE:
- tasks/todo.md and tasks/lessons.md at monorepo root
- Root AGENT.md Workflow Rules table
- docs/EXECUTION-RULES.md with MUST and MUST NOT lists
- backend/agents/AGENT.md Subagent Execution Rules
- CI enforcement: check-workflow-docs step in ci.yml

MILESTONE: MVP 1 — Scraper Foundation & In-Memory Store

Create the tasks/ directory and tasks/todo.md at the monorepo root to track the active implementation plan.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create tasks/ directory at monorepo root
- tasks/todo.md structure:
  - ## Active Plan header with current date and active epic/step
  - Checkable step list: - [ ] Step X.Y: description
  - ## Review section appended on step completion: timestamp, what was verified, evidence command and output
- Initial content: populate with Phase 1 Epic 1 steps as first active plan
- Add tasks/ to .gitignore exclusions check: tasks/ must NOT be gitignored (it is tracked)

EFFORT: S (1 hour)
PROJECT AREA: Process
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- tasks/todo.md exists at monorepo root
- File contains Active Plan header and at least one checkable step
- Review section format documented with example
- File is tracked by git (not in .gitignore)

EDGE CASES:
- Ensure tasks/ directory committed via placeholder file if initially empty

Create tasks/lessons.md to capture mistake patterns, root causes, and prevention rules across implementation sessions.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- tasks/lessons.md structure:
  - ## Session: YYYY-MM-DD header per session
  - ### Mistake: short label
  - Root Cause: one sentence
  - Rule to Prevent: one actionable rule (imperative sentence)
- Initial content: seed with one example entry documenting a common AI agent mistake pattern (e.g. implementing before confirming plan)
- lessons.md reviewed at start of every new implementation session per EXECUTION-RULES.md

EFFORT: S (1 hour)
PROJECT AREA: Process
DEPENDENCIES: JD-16-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- tasks/lessons.md exists at monorepo root
- File contains at least one seeded example entry in correct format
- File is tracked by git

EDGE CASES:
- File must remain human-readable plain markdown; no JSON or YAML frontmatter

Update the root AGENT.md index file to include the Workflow Rules table and reference tasks/ files.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add ## Workflow Rules section to root AGENT.md with table columns: Rule | Behaviour
- Rules to document:
  - Plan Mode: confirm plan before any implementation
  - Task Log: append to tasks/todo.md before starting each step
  - Verify Plan: run verification command before marking step done
  - Subagents: offload research/exploration/parallel analysis to subagents
  - Lessons Review: read tasks/lessons.md at session start
  - Correction Loop: on error, capture in lessons.md before retrying
  - Done Gate: step not done until verification evidence recorded
  - Elegance Check: pause before submitting; consider simpler approach
  - Bug Reports: fix bugs autonomously; document in tasks/lessons.md
- Update ## Where to look index to include tasks/todo.md and tasks/lessons.md entries

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Process
DEPENDENCIES: JD-16-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Root AGENT.md contains Workflow Rules table with all 9 rules
- Where to look index updated with tasks/ entries
- Markdown renders correctly with no broken table formatting

EDGE CASES:
- AGENT.md must remain the single root index; do not create a separate workflow file at root level

Create docs/EXECUTION-RULES.md documenting the mandatory MUST and MUST NOT rules for AI agent execution.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create docs/ directory if not already present
- docs/EXECUTION-RULES.md sections:
  - ## Workflow Execution Rules
  - ### MUST list (9 items):
    1. Enter plan mode and confirm before implementing
    2. Append step to tasks/todo.md before starting
    3. Check in with human after each epic completion
    4. Review tasks/lessons.md at session start
    5. Capture mistakes in tasks/lessons.md immediately
    6. Mark steps done incrementally with verification evidence
    7. Include verification command output as evidence
    8. Pause for elegance check before submitting large PRs
    9. Fix bugs autonomously and document fix in lessons.md
  - ### MUST NOT list (5 items):
    1. Implement before plan confirmed
    2. Mark step done without verification evidence
    3. Apply hacky fixes (document and raise instead)
    4. Edit files outside current step scope
    5. Repeat a mistake already captured in lessons.md

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Process
DEPENDENCIES: JD-16-3

TESTING CRITERIA:
- docs/EXECUTION-RULES.md exists with both MUST and MUST NOT sections
- All 9 MUST rules present
- All 5 MUST NOT rules present
- Document cross-references root AGENT.md Workflow Rules table

EDGE CASES:
- Keep rules imperative and unambiguous; avoid vague language like 'try to' or 'consider'

Update backend/agents/AGENT.md to include the Subagent Execution Rules governing how agents offload work.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add ## Subagent Execution Rules section to backend/agents/AGENT.md
- Rules to document:
  - One task per subagent invocation: no multi-task subagent calls
  - Offload strategy: use subagents for research, exploration, parallel analysis, and eval runs
  - Context window reservation: subagent must summarise result to under 500 tokens before returning to orchestrator
  - Result summarisation protocol: return structured summary not raw output
  - Escalation on subagent failure: log error, mark step blocked in tasks/todo.md, notify human
- Add ## How to Add a New Scraper section with step-by-step template instructions

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Process
DEPENDENCIES: JD-3-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
- backend/agents/AGENT.md contains Subagent Execution Rules section with all 5 rules
- How to Add a New Scraper section present with actionable steps
- Document references @register decorator and BaseScrapeAgent ABC

EDGE CASES:
- AGENT.md must be written for both human developers and AI agents reading the file as context

DEPENDENCIES: JD-5-4, JD-16-2

TESTING CRITERIA:
